assertions.description: The analyzer correctly identifies 29 total components: 5 resistors (R1-R5), 2 capacitors (C1, C2), 1 LED (D1), 1 IC (U1 LIS3DH), 3 jumpers (JP1-JP3), 5 connectors (J1-J5), 4 fiducials (FID1-FID4), 4 mounting holes (ST1-ST4), and 4 aesthetic/logo symbols. 19 nets, 0 no-connects, and power rails 3.3V/GND are all correctly reported.

The Qwiic 1x1 design has two Qwiic connectors (J3 and J5) plus a standard 5-pin PTH header (J2) and a 4-pin I2C header (J4). The analyzer correctly identifies J3 and J5 as Qwiic connectors (JST 1mm 4-pin) and J4 as an I2C connector. Both Qwiic connectors are on the same I2C bus, enabling daisy-chaining.

INCORRECT: The Qwiic 1x1 design has R3 (2.2k) on SCL and R2 (2.2k) on SDA as I2C pull-ups, connected through JP2 (closed jumper) to 3.3V. The analyzer reports has_pullup=false and pull_ups=[] for both I2C lines. The resistors are visible in the net topology (R3 pin 1 on SCL net, R2 pin 1 on SDA net) and their other pins connect to unnamed nets that feed through JP2 to 3.3V. The indirect path through the jumper prevents the pull-up from being detected.

INCORRECT: All 24 unique component references appear in missing_mpn, which is accurate: the SparkFun custom library symbols do not embed MPN values. The analyzer correctly reports this. This is a design data quality observation, not an analyzer error.
